methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $50M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $500M - $32.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: Safe Superintelligence
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series B), post-money $32B

private.name: Physical Intelligence
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series B), post-money $5.6B

private.name: Nominal

private.name: Photoroom
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series B), post-money $500M

data.narrative:
Reality Defender is a Series B AI & Software company founded by Ben Colman that has raised $50M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of 5 same-stage, same-sector private companies (Safe Superintelligence, Physical Intelligence, World Labs, Nominal, and Photoroom) alongside one public comparable (PLTR).

Two methods anchor the valuation. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.9x multiplier typical of Series B advancement to the $50M total raised proxy, implying $95M. Peer-median analysis of the five comparable Series B AI & Software companies yields a median valuation of $5.0B, with the peer range spanning $500M to $32.0B. The wide peer dispersion reflects heterogeneity in market positions and investor appetite within the frontier AI subsector.

Triangulating these methods produces a valuation range of $81M to $5.8B, with a median point of $2.5B. The stage-progression method floors the range conservatively; the peer-median method anchors the upper bound. Key uncertainties include the appropriate positioning of Reality Defender within the peer distribution—particularly given the 64x gap between minimum and maximum peer valuations—and the extent to which frontier AI valuations reflect durable revenue multiples versus speculative capital flows. Revenue data and unit economics would narrow this range materially.
